back_translation: At that time, the tax collectors and sinners were gathering to come near to Him in order to listen to Him.
The Pharisees and the scribes grumbled together, saying, “This man welcomes sinners, and he eats food together with them.”
Jesus therefore told this parable to them, saying,
“Which man among you, who has a hundred sheep, and one of them is lost, would not leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness and go out to search for the one that is lost until he finds it?
And when he has found it, he lifts that sheep and places it upon his shoulders with joy,
and when he returns to the house, he calls friends and neighbors to come together, saying to them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep that was lost.’
I tell you that in the same way, there will be joy in heaven because of one sinner who repents, more than because of ninety-nine righteous persons who do not need to repent.
Or what woman, having ten silver coins, and she loses one coin, would not light a lamp, sweep the house, and search carefully and thoroughly, until she finds it?
And when she has found it, she calls her women friends and neighbors to come together, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found the coin that I had lost.’
I tell you that in the same way, there is joy before the angels of God because of one sinner who repents.”
He said again, “A certain man had two sons.
The younger son spoke to the father, saying, ‘Father, sir, please grant to me the portion of property that I am to receive as my inheritance.’ So the father divided his possessions to both of them.
Not many days later, the younger son gathered everything he had, set out on a journey to a distant land, and there he squandered his possessions by living wastefully.
When he had spent everything, a severe famine arose throughout that land, and he began to be in want.
So he went to reside and work with a citizen of that land, who sent him out into his fields to feed pigs.
He longed to take the bean pods that the pigs were eating and eat them to fill his stomach, but no one gave anything to him at all.
But when he came to his senses, he mused to himself, ‘How much food to spare my father’s hired servants have, yet here I am, about to die of hunger.
I will rise up and go back to my father, and I will say to him, "Father, sir, I have sinned against the heavens and against you.
I am no longer worthy to be called your son. Please make me like one of your hired servants."’
So he rose up and went back to his own father. While he was still far away, his father saw him and had a compassionate, pitying heart; he ran to embrace his neck and kissed him with love.
The son then said to the father, ‘Father, sir, I have sinned against the heavens and against you; I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’
But the father commanded his servants, ‘Quickly, bring the best robe and clothe him with it, and bring a ring and put it on his finger, and bring sandals and put them on his feet.
And bring the fattened calf that was fattened and kill it; let us eat and feast and rejoice.
Because this son of mine was dead but has come back to life; he was lost and is found again.’ Then they began to feast.
At that time, his older son was in the field, and when he came back and was near the house, he heard the sound of music and the sound of dancing.
So he called one of the young servants to come and asked what the matter was.
The young servant answered him, ‘Your younger brother has returned, and your father has ordered the fattened calf to be killed, because he has gotten his younger son back safely.’
The older son became angry and refused to enter the house. The father therefore came out and entreated him.

He answered his father, ‘Behold, throughout all these many years, I have served you like a slave and have never disobeyed any of your commands; yet you have never given me even a young goat, even one, so that I might celebrate with my friends.
But when this son of yours, the one who squandered your property with prostitutes, has come back, you have ordered the fattened calf to be killed for him.’
The father then said to him, ‘Child, you have always been with me, and everything that is mine is yours.
But it is fitting to celebrate and rejoice, because this younger brother of yours was dead but has come back to life; he was lost and is found again.’”
